info.title: rust-brain control API
info.description: Control-plane API for rust-brain: auth, tenant management, API key, GitHub integration, and agent session endpoints.

get.summary: List admin audit-log rows (operator-only, ADR-012 §S1.2).
get.description:
Returns up to `limit` rows (default 100, max 500) in descending
`created_at` order.  Each row now includes the `ip` and `user_agent`
fields that were captured at request time (RUSAA-1801).

parameters.name: tenant_id
parameters.description: Filter to a specific tenant; omit for global view.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum rows to return. Defaults to 100; max 500.

401.description: Missing or invalid admin bearer token

get.summary: Liveness probe with per-store connectivity status.
get.description:
Returns 200 in all cases (even when stores are degraded) so load-balancers
do not kill the process — callers inspect `status` for fine-grained health.
Public / unauthenticated.

get.summary: Compile-time build provenance — SHA, timestamp, and dirty flag.
get.description:
Public / unauthenticated. Returns commit SHA only (board decision 2026-05-21).
Used by CI smoke gate and the dev-stack watcher to detect stale images.

get.summary: Readiness probe — returns 200 when the service is ready to serve traffic.
Build identity — SHA and timestamp baked in at image build time.
get.description:
Public / unauthenticated. Returns `"unknown"` for fields not set during
the docker build (e.g. local `docker build` without `--build-arg`).

302.description: Redirect to FE admin success page

403.description: Caller is not a platform admin
Caller is not a platform admin
Caller is not a platform admin

get.summary: List all agent sessions for the current session's tenant.
get.description:
Returns sessions ordered by `created_at DESC`.
Requires an active session.

get.summary: Get a single agent session by ID.
get.description:
Performs a two-step lookup (matching `delete_session`'s pattern) so that
a cross-tenant access attempt yields 403 instead of 404. The query selects
by `id` only; tenant ownership is then verified in application code.

404.description: Session not found

403.description: Not your session, or API key lacks the `agent` scope

404.description: Session not found

parameters.name: from_sequence
parameters.description: Sequence number to replay history from

403.description: Insufficient permissions to access this session

404.description: Session not found

get.summary: `GET /v1/agents/sessions/{id}/events/history`
get.description:
Returns a paged slice of `agents.agent_events` for the given session.
Uses a `limit + 1` probe to determine whether a next page exists without
a separate COUNT query.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Exclusive lower bound on sequence (omit for beginning)

404.description: Session not found

get.summary: `GET /v1/agents/sessions/{id}/log.ndjson`
get.description:
Streams every row in `agents.agent_events` for the given session as
newline-delimited JSON ordered by `sequence ASC`.

parameters.name: raw
parameters.description: Set to '1' to bypass redaction (requires admin scope)

403.description: Insufficient permissions to access this session

404.description: Session not found

get.summary: List all active (non-revoked) API keys for the current session's tenant.
get.description:
Plaintext keys are never returned — only metadata.
Requires an active session.

post.summary: Create a new API key for the current session's tenant.
post.description:
The plaintext key is returned exactly once in the `key` field.
Subsequent reads from `GET /v1/api-keys` will not include the plaintext.
Requires an active session.

400.description: Missing or empty name or scopes

delete.description:
Any authenticated member of the tenant may revoke any key belonging to
that tenant. Revocation is immediate and irreversible.
Requires an active session.

404.description: Key not found or already revoked

get.summary: List audit events (admin only).
get.description:
Requires an `Admin`-scoped API key **or** an active session with at least
the `admin` tenant role.  Returns 403 for any other caller.

Session callers may only query their own tenant's events; the `tenant_id`
query parameter, if provided, must match the session tenant.  API key
callers may pass any `tenant_id` (or omit it to scope to the key's tenant).

parameters.name: tenant_id
parameters.description:
Restrict to this tenant UUID.  For session callers this must match
(or be absent) — the session tenant is used as the implicit default.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of rows to return (1–500; default 100).

post.description:
Always returns 200 OK regardless of whether the address is registered,
preventing email enumeration. When found, a reset link with a 15-minute
expiry is emailed. When not found, a dummy argon2id hash is performed to
keep the response time within ±50ms of a real lookup.

200.description: Reset email sent or silently skipped

post.summary: Authenticate with email and password, creating a new session.
post.description:
Verifies credentials with argon2id, creates a 30-day sliding-expiry session,
and sets an `HttpOnly` `rb_session` cookie. Rate-limited to 5 failures per
10-minute window; exceeding the threshold returns 429 for 15 minutes.

post.summary: Revoke the caller's current session and clear the `rb_session` cookie.
post.description:
Sets `revoked_at = now()` on the session row, writes a `logout` row to
`control.auth_events` for audit, and returns `204 No Content` with a
`Set-Cookie` header that overwrites `rb_session` with `Max-Age=0` so the
browser drops it. Requires an authenticated session — anonymous and
API-key callers receive `401 unauthorized`.

Invalidates any existing unused verify tokens for the address and issues a
fresh one with a 1-hour expiry. Always returns 204 to avoid email
enumeration — callers cannot distinguish a known-unverified address from
an unknown or already-verified one. Rate-gated via the login rate limiter.

post.summary: Consume a reset token and set a new password.
post.description:
Marks the token used, updates the password hash, and revokes **all** active
sessions for the user. The caller must re-authenticate after resetting.

204.description: Password updated and all sessions revoked

post.summary: Register a new user and create their first tenant workspace.
Verify an email address using a single-use token.
post.description:
Accepts the plaintext token from the verification email, looks up its
SHA-256 hash in `email_tokens`, validates that it is unused and unexpired,
then sets `users.email_verified_at` and marks the token used. An
`email_verified` auth event is written atomically in the same transaction.

400.description: Token expired, already used, or not found (invalid_token)

404.description: Feature not enabled
Session not found or feature disabled
Session not found or feature disabled
Session not found or feature disabled

parameters.name: after_seq
parameters.description: Cursor: return messages after this seq

404.description: Session not found or feature disabled
Session not found or feature disabled

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Opaque state token from install-url (absent for GitHub-initiated redirects)

503.description: GitHub App not configured on this instance
GitHub App not configured on this instance

404.description: Installation not found or not owned by this tenant

409.description: Installation belongs to a deactivated App (installation_for_different_app)

503.description: GitHub App not configured on this instance

post.summary: Execute an arbitrary Cypher query against the tenant's Neo4j graph store.
post.description:
The tenant label is automatically injected into every node pattern so queries
are isolated to the calling tenant's data. Multi-statement queries
(containing a bare semicolon outside strings/comments) are rejected.

When `read_only` is `true` (the default), the query is pre-flight checked
for Cypher write operators (`CREATE`, `MERGE`, `SET`, `DELETE`, `DETACH`,
`REMOVE`). Any match causes a `400 cypher_write_denied` response before the
query reaches Neo4j.

Requires an API key with the `admin` scope **or** an active session with the
`owner` or `admin` tenant role.

400.description: Malformed query or write operators detected in read-only mode (cypher_write_denied / invalid_input)

get.summary: Kafka consistency metrics (admin only).
get.description:
Reports consumer lag and time since last event for each data-plane store.
Admin-only because these metrics expose internal pipeline internals.

Requires an `Admin`-scoped API key **or** an active session with at least
the `admin` tenant role.

get.summary:
Confirms that the GitHub App private key is valid and matches what GitHub
knows. Uses a 60-second server-side cache to be safe for liveness probes.
get.description: Returns 503 when `RB_GH_APP_ID` / `RB_GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY` are not set.

get.summary: List recent ingestion runs for the caller's tenant.
get.description:
Returns up to `limit` runs ordered newest-first.  Used by the
Activity Dashboard (REQ-FE-07) and Trace Viewer index (REQ-FE-08).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of runs to return (1–100; default 50).

403.description: Email not verified or insufficient scope

get.summary: Retrieve the stage timeline for an ingestion run (trace viewer fallback).
get.description:
Reads `pipeline_stage_runs` ordered by stage sequence.  The cross-tenant
isolation check joins `ingestion_runs` to verify `tenant_id` matches the
caller's session/key.  Returns 404 when the run does not exist or belongs
to a different tenant.

403.description: Email not verified or insufficient scope

404.description: Ingestion run not found or belongs to another tenant

get.summary:
Return the authenticated user's profile, current tenant, and available
tenants. As a side-effect, refreshes the session's `last_seen_at` and
extends `expires_at` by `session_ttl_days` so the session is sliding
rather than fixed-from-login. The refresh is fire-and-forget — failing
to extend the session does not fail the request (see REQ-AU-06).

post.summary: Switch the active tenant for the current session.
post.description:
The caller must already be a member of the target tenant. The session's
`tenant_id` is updated in place and a `tenant_switched` auth event is
written. Returns the new active tenant with the caller's role.
Requires: verified session.

403.description: Email not verified (email_not_verified) or not a member (not_a_member)

404.description: Target tenant not found or inactive

get.summary: List all connected repositories for the current session's tenant.
get.description:
Soft-deleted repos (`archived_at IS NOT NULL`) are excluded.
Results are ordered by `connected_at DESC` (most recently connected first).
Requires a verified session.

post.summary: Connect a GitHub repository to the calling user's active tenant.
post.description:
Verifies the installation belongs to the session tenant, confirms the repo
is accessible via GitHub's API, then inserts a `repos` row with
`status = 'connected'`.

404.description: Installation not found or not owned by this tenant

409.description: Repository already connected (repo_already_connected) OR installation belongs to a deactivated App (installation_for_different_app)

503.description: GitHub App not configured on this instance

post.summary: Trigger an asynchronous ingestion run for a connected repository.
post.description:
Returns 202 immediately; ingestion is processed asynchronously by the worker.
404 if the repository does not exist or belongs to another tenant.
409 if an ingestion run is already queued or running for this repo.
503 if the Kafka producer is unavailable.

404.description: Repository not found or belongs to another tenant

post.summary: Trigger a manual ingestion run for a connected repository.
post.description:
Creates an `ingestion_runs` row (status `queued`) and one
`pipeline_stage_runs` row per stage (status `pending`), then publishes an
`IngestRequest` to `rb.ingest.clone.commands`.

Returns 409 if an ingestion is already queued or running for this repo.
Returns 503 if the Kafka producer is unavailable.

404.description: Repository not found or belongs to another tenant

get.summary: Retrieve a code symbol by its fully-qualified name within a repository.
get.description:
`fqn_b64` must be the FQN encoded as URL-safe base64 without padding
(`base64url` / RFC 4648 §5, no `=` padding). Returns the item's metadata
and, when the stored source is ≤ 4 KiB, an inline `source_preview`.
Larger items carry only a `blob_ref` URI pointing to the full AST JSON.

Cross-tenant requests are rejected: the repository must belong to the
caller's tenant (AC4). Returns 404 both when the repo is absent and when
the `(repo_id, fqn)` tuple is not found (AC2).

parameters.name: fqn_b64
parameters.description: URL-safe base64 (no padding) encoded fully-qualified name

403.description: Email not verified or API key lacks read scope

404.description: Repository or item not found (not_found)

get.summary: List all functions transitively called by the target item (callees BFS).
get.description:
Traverses `CALLS` and `CALL_INSTANTIATES` edges forward from the root.
See the callers endpoint for provenance semantics and pagination.

parameters.name: fqn_b64
parameters.description: URL-safe base64 (no padding) encoded item FQN

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum edges to return per page (1–200, default 50).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque continuation cursor from a prior response.

400.description: Malformed fqn_b64, depth > 10, or invalid cursor

403.description: Email not verified or insufficient scope

404.description: Repository not found or belongs to another tenant

503.description: Neo4j graph not configured on this instance

get.summary: List all functions that transitively call the target item (callers BFS).
get.description:
Traverses `CALLS` and `CALL_INSTANTIATES` edges backward from the root.
Cycle detection prevents infinite loops; per-edge provenance distinguishes
static (`direct`), monomorphized (`monomorph`), and dynamic (`dyn_candidate`) calls.
Use `next_cursor` for pagination.

parameters.name: fqn_b64
parameters.description: URL-safe base64 (no padding) encoded item FQN

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum edges to return per page (1–200, default 50).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque continuation cursor from a prior response.

400.description: Malformed fqn_b64, depth > 10, or invalid cursor

403.description: Email not verified or insufficient scope

404.description: Repository not found or belongs to another tenant

503.description: Neo4j graph not configured on this instance

get.summary: List all impl blocks for the trait identified by `fqn_b64` within a repository.
get.description:
Returns both direct impls (`impl Trait for Type`) and blanket impls
(`impl<T: Bound> Trait for T`) found in the graph.

`fqn_b64` must be URL-safe base64 (no padding) of the trait's FQN.
Requires the graph to be configured (`RB_NEO4J_URI`); returns 503 otherwise.

parameters.name: fqn_b64
parameters.description: URL-safe base64 (no padding) encoded trait FQN

403.description: Email not verified or insufficient scope

404.description: Repository not found or belongs to another tenant

503.description: Neo4j graph not configured on this instance

get.summary: List all usages of the type identified by `fqn_b64` within a repository.
get.description:
Returns two categories of usage combined in a flat list:
- **Textual** (`usage_kind = "textual"`)  — items that reference the type
  by name (`USES_TYPE` edge).
- **Monomorphized** (`usage_kind = "monomorphized"`) — `TypeInstance` nodes
  derived from this type via a `MONOMORPHIZED_FROM` edge.

`fqn_b64` must be URL-safe base64 (no padding) of the type's FQN.
Requires the graph to be configured (`RB_NEO4J_URI`); returns 503 otherwise.

parameters.name: fqn_b64
parameters.description: URL-safe base64 (no padding) encoded type FQN

403.description: Email not verified or insufficient scope

404.description: Repository not found or belongs to another tenant

503.description: Neo4j graph not configured on this instance

get.summary: Return the crate/module hierarchy for a repository.
get.description:
Tree is derived from `code_symbols.fqn` (split on `::`) via a single SQL
query. Results are cached in-process for 60 s per `(repo_id, last_ingest_run_id)`.

403.description: Email not verified or insufficient scope

404.description: Repository not found or belongs to another tenant

post.summary: Semantic search across embedded code symbols within the caller's tenant.
post.description:
Embeds `q` via Ollama, performs approximate nearest-neighbour search in
the Qdrant `rb_embeddings` collection filtered by `tenant_id`, and returns
ranked results with their fully-qualified names and crate context.

Returns 503 when either Qdrant (`RB_QDRANT_URL`) or Ollama (`RB_OLLAMA_URL`)
are not configured on this instance.

400.description: Invalid request (query empty or limit out of range)

403.description: Email not verified or API key lacks read scope

503.description: Qdrant or Ollama not configured on this instance

delete.summary: Delete a tenant (owner-only, requires typed confirmation).
delete.description:
Soft-deletes the tenant by setting `deleted_at` and transitioning its
status to `deleting`, cancels all in-flight ingestion runs, then emits a
`Tombstone` to `rb.tombstones.v1`. The tombstoner service performs the
async data-plane cleanup (`PostgreSQL` schema drop, `Neo4j` node removal,
`Qdrant` point deletion).

**Idempotent**: repeating the call on a tenant already in `deleting` or
`deleted` state returns `204 No Content`.

**Typed confirmation**: the `X-Confirm` header must match the tenant slug
exactly (case-insensitive) to prevent accidental deletions.

Returns `503` if the Kafka producer is not available — the request can be
retried once the broker is reachable.

400.description: X-Confirm header missing or slug mismatch (confirmation_mismatch)

404.description: Tenant not found

get.summary: List all members of a tenant.
get.description:
Returns the user ID, email, role, and invitation time for every member.
Requires: session with at least member role in the target tenant.

post.summary: Invite a user to this tenant by email.
post.description:
If the user already has an account they are added immediately with the
`member` role. If they do not yet have an account, an invite email is sent
with a signup link.
Requires: session with admin or owner role in the target tenant.

403.description: Not a member or insufficient role (not_a_member / insufficient_role)

delete.summary: Remove a member from a tenant.
delete.description:
Cannot remove the owner. The removed member's active sessions for this
tenant are immediately revoked.
Requires: session with admin or owner role.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: User ID of the member to remove

404.description: Member not found

put.summary: Change a member's role within a tenant.
put.description:
Requires: session with admin or owner role.
Cannot change the owner's role — use transfer-ownership for that.
Cannot set `owner` as the new role — use transfer-ownership.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: User ID of the member to update

400.description: Cannot demote owner (cannot_remove_owner) or invalid role

404.description: Member not found

post.summary: Transfer the owner role to another existing member.
post.description:
Atomically sets the current owner to `admin` and the target to `owner`.
The target must already be a member of the tenant.
Requires: session with owner role.

404.description: Target user is not a member

AppManifestRequest.description:
Body of `POST /v1/admin/github/app-manifest`.

`name` is operator-supplied (Q2: CTO 2026-05-12). When absent, defaults to
`rustacean-{RB_DEPLOYMENT_ID}` (fallback `rustacean-dev`).

name.description: GitHub-facing App name — visible on the consent screen.

redirect_url.description:
`https://github.com/settings/apps/new?manifest=…&state=…` —
platform admin should be redirected here.

state_token.description:
Opaque state token returned in the GitHub callback. Persisted as a
sha256 hash; this hex string is single-use and short-lived.

AppSource.description: Source identifying where the active App credentials came from.

app_id.description: Numeric GitHub App ID — present only when `configured == true`.

configured.description: True when a `GhApp` is currently loaded into `state.gh_loader`.

installed_at.description: Install timestamp — present only for DB-sourced configurations.

installed_by.description:
Platform-admin user that installed the App — present only for
DB-sourced configurations.

slug.description: GitHub App slug — present only for DB-sourced configurations.

source.description: Where the active App came from.

ip.description: Client IP address extracted from `X-Forwarded-For` or peer address (nullable).

dirty.description: `"true"` when the working tree had uncommitted changes at build time.

sha.description:
Compile-time git SHA baked into the binary.  `"unknown"` when built
without `RB_BUILD_SHA` in the environment (e.g. ad-hoc local builds).

timestamp.description: Commit timestamp captured at compile time (`RB_BUILD_TIMESTAMP`).

default_branch.description: Default branch override. If omitted, the value is fetched from GitHub.

github_repo_id.description: GitHub numeric repository ID (from the list-repos response).

installation_id.description: Internal installation UUID (from the GitHub App install redirect).

scopes.description: Scopes the key is authorized to use.

key.description: Plaintext key — shown exactly once. Store it securely; it cannot be retrieved later.

workspace_path.description: Optional override for workspace sub-path; defaults to `tenant_id/session_id`.

email.description: Email address for the account to recover.

cypher.description:
Raw Cypher statement. The tenant label is injected automatically; multi-statement
queries (semicolons outside strings) are rejected.

params.description: Named parameters bound into the query (`$key` → value).

read_only.description:
When `true` (default), the query is pre-flight checked for write operators
(CREATE, MERGE, SET, DELETE, DETACH, REMOVE) and rejected with 400 if found.

rows.description: Each element is a JSON object mapping column names to their values.

status.description: Overall status: `"ok"` when all stores are reachable, `"degraded"` otherwise.

ImplEntry.description: One impl block that implements the queried trait.

fqn.description: Fully-qualified name of the impl block.

impl_kind.description: `"direct"` for concrete impls; `"blanket"` for blanket impls.

impls.description: Impl blocks found in the graph.

state_token.description: The raw opaque state token embedded in the URL.

url.description: Full GitHub App install URL to open in the browser.

email.description: Email address of the user to invite or add.

invited.description: `true` when the invite email was sent (user did not have an existing account).

user_id.description: Present when an existing user was added directly.

ItemResponse.description: Code symbol returned by the item-lookup endpoint.

blob_ref.description:
`rb-blob://` URI for the item's serialised AST JSON.
Populated only when the item's source exceeds 4 KiB (AC3).

line_end.description: 1-based end line of the item in `source_path`.

line_start.description: 1-based start line of the item in `source_path`.

source_preview.description:
Inline source text — present when the item's source is ≤ 4 KiB.
Absent when `blob_ref` is populated (AC3).

email_verification_required.description: `true` when `email_verified_at IS NULL` — caller should redirect to verification.

MeResponse.description:
Response body of `GET /v1/me`: who the caller is, what tenant they are
currently scoped to, and every other tenant available to them.

ModuleNodeItem.description: A single node in the module/item tree.

name.description: Leaf segment of the FQN (e.g. `"Vec"` for `alloc::vec::Vec`).

oneOf.description: Source location — absent for virtual/synthetic module nodes.

trace_id.description: 32-hex OpenTelemetry trace ID.  `null` when not yet propagated.

email.description: Email address of the account awaiting verification.

token.description: Plaintext reset token from the emailed link.

Scope.description: Access scope for an API key.

SearchFilters.description: Optional filters applied on top of the vector similarity ranking.

repo_id.description: Restrict results to a single repository UUID.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 10, max 50).

q.description: Natural-language query to embed and search.

SearchResult.description: A single ranked result returned by `/v1/search`.

crate_name.description: Top-level crate name extracted from the FQN.

repo_id.description: Repository UUID this symbol belongs to.

tenant_name.description: Display name for the new tenant workspace.

email_verification_required.description: Always `true` on signup — email verification required before tenant access.

tenant_id.description: UUID of the tenant to switch to.

TenantWithRole.description: One tenant the caller is a member of, with their role.

user_id.description: User ID of the existing member to promote to owner.

TraversalEdgeSchema.description: A directed edge in the call graph.

TraversalNodeSchema.description: A node discovered during BFS traversal.

branch.description:
Target branch name. Optional; clone stage falls back to the repo's
`default_branch` when both `commit_sha` and `branch` are absent.

commit_sha.description:
Target commit SHA. Optional; if omitted the clone stage resolves the
branch HEAD.

trace_id.description: 32-hex OpenTelemetry trace ID for this run. `null` when no active trace context.

role.description: Target role: `member` or `admin`. Cannot set `owner` via this endpoint.

UsageEntry.description: One item that references the queried type.

fqn.description: Fully-qualified name of the using item or type instance.

usage_kind.description:
`"textual"` — item uses the type by name in its source.
`"monomorphized"` — `TypeInstance` instantiated from this type's `TypeDef`.

usages.description: All usages found in the graph, combining textual and monomorphized.

UserInfo.description: Public profile of the authenticated user.

token.description: Plaintext verification token from the emailed link.

tags.name: chat
tags.description: Chat panel — session lifecycle and message stream (ADR-013)
